tagline: A local-first explorer for MIMIC-III clinical notes — a human-in-the-loop clinical workflow built on MCP
versionBadge: Version {{version}} · data stays on this device
intro: NoteSentry is a fully local tool for exploring and working with clinical notes. You ask questions in plain language, and a local large language model queries a local SQLite database through Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools, then composes the answer. All inference runs on local Ollama — nothing is uploaded, no telemetry, no outbound connections — so clinical text can be explored efficiently without ever leaving the machine.
privacyDesc: This data is governed by the PhysioNet Credentialed Data Use Agreement (DUA) and may not be shared with any third party. NoteSentry applies three layers of defense — local inference, human review, and full auditing — to safeguard both the data and clinical accountability.

data.title: Data · Grounding
data.desc: The model can only read real local data through MCP tools, keeping answers grounded and reducing factual hallucinations.

human.title: Human · Review
human.desc: Every tool call requires a clinician's explicit approval — the final gate on clinical decisions is never skipped.

audit.title: Audit · Trace
audit.desc: The time, tool, parameters, and approval of every call are fully recorded and traceable.

school.name: National Taipei University of Nursing and Health Sciences
